title: Danheiser Cyclopentene Annulation – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Danheiser cyclopentene annulation describing TiCl4-promoted [3+2] annulation of trimethylsilylallenes with electron-deficient alkenes (allenophiles) to form highly substituted cyclopentenes through allylic cation capture and 1,2-silyl shift pathways.
notes: Key concepts: TiCl4 activation of enones, trimethylsilylallene addition, allylic carbocation intermediates, 1,2-silyl shift, highly substituted cyclopentene formation.

title: Danheiser Cyclopentene Annulation – Synthetic Applications
summary: Application page for the Danheiser cyclopentene annulation featuring disilane variants and complex annulations in syntheses of bicyclic ketones, clavukerin and isoclavukerin intermediates, and cis-substituted dihydrofurans from aldehyde-derived heteroallenophile partners.
notes: Examples highlight bicyclic ring construction, disilanyl annulation chemistry, and high stereoselectivity in cyclic enone substrates.

title: Danishefsky's Diene Cycloaddition – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for Danishefsky's diene cycloaddition describing the electron-rich 1-methoxy-3-trimethylsilyloxy-1,3-butadiene as a versatile heterodiene in Lewis-acid-mediated or thermal [4+2] cycloadditions, often proceeding through Mukaiyama-aldol or Diels–Alder manifolds to oxygenated cyclohexenones and dihydropyrones.
notes: Key concepts: Danishefsky diene, hetero Diels–Alder cycloaddition, Mukaiyama aldol pathway, Lewis-acid activation, oxygenated six-membered rings.

title: Danishefsky's Diene Cycloaddition – Synthetic Applications
summary: Application page for Danishefsky's diene cycloaddition featuring regioselective annulations in syntheses of tubipofuran, (-)-phyllanthine, (+/-)-A80915G, and fluorinated dihydropyrone building blocks from quinone or cyclic imine partners.
notes: Examples emphasize Lewis-acid-promoted hetero Diels–Alder reactions with quinones, imines, and fluorinated aldehydes.

title: Darzens Glycidic Ester Condensation – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Darzens glycidic ester condensation describing base-promoted reaction of aldehydes or ketones with alpha-halo esters, nitriles, sulfones, or amides to form glycidic esters or related epoxides through enolate addition and intramolecular SN2 ring closure.
notes: Key concepts: alpha-halo carbonyl pronucleophiles, Darzens condensation, halohydrin/enolate intermediates, glycidic ester formation, cis/trans epoxide control.

title: Darzens Glycidic Ester Condensation – Synthetic Applications
summary: Application page for the Darzens glycidic ester condensation featuring stereocontrolled spiro-epoxide formation in coriolin synthesis, epoxides used en route to epiasarinin, and auxiliary-controlled asymmetric Darzens reactions in diltiazem synthesis.
notes: Examples emphasize stereochemical control through enolate geometry, bromohydrin generation, and chiral auxiliary induction.

title: Davis' Oxaziridine Oxidations – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for Davis' oxaziridine oxidations describing N-sulfonyloxaziridines as neutral oxygen-transfer reagents for sulfide and selenide oxidation, amine N-oxidation, and especially alpha-hydroxylation of enolates to acyloins via SN2-type oxygen transfer.
notes: Key concepts: oxaziridine oxygen transfer, Davis reagent family, enolate alpha-hydroxylation, sulfide oxidation, non-chelated transition-state control.

title: Davis' Oxaziridine Oxidations – Synthetic Applications
summary: Application page for Davis' oxaziridine oxidations featuring alpha-hydroxylation steps in syntheses of epothilone B, okadaic-acid domains, and (-)-fumiquinazoline natural products from chiral oxazolidinone-derived or lactone enolates.
notes: Examples highlight stereocontrolled alpha-oxygenation of complex enolates and downstream elaboration to advanced natural-product fragments.

title: De Mayo Cycloaddition (Enone-Alkene [2+2] Photocycloaddition) – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the De Mayo cycloaddition describing photochemical [2+2] cycloaddition between enones and alkenes to generate cyclobutanes or, via beta-hydroxy ketone intermediates and retro-aldol fragmentation, 1,5-diketones. The page emphasizes triplet-state exciplexes, 1,4-biradicals, and topology-dependent regioselectivity.
notes: Key concepts: triplet enone photochemistry, enone-alkene [2+2] cycloaddition, beta-hydroxy ketone intermediates, retro-aldol fragmentation, substituted cyclobutane formation.

title: De Mayo Cycloaddition (Enone-Alkene [2+2] Photocycloaddition) – Synthetic Applications
summary: Application page for the De Mayo cycloaddition featuring intermolecular and intramolecular [2+2] photochemistry combined with fragmentation, SmI2-mediated ring opening, and radical elaboration in syntheses of taxane frameworks, guanacastepene skeletons, ingenol, and (+)-alismol.
notes: Examples emphasize photochemical cyclobutane formation followed by fragmentation or retro-aldol unmasking to access complex bridged and medium-ring carbon frameworks.
